  "findings": [
    "Flashing is not lapped or sealed at joints, allowing water penetration behind the masonry veneer."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop work on veneer closure, peel back masonry as needed, and properly lap and seal all flashing joints using approved mastic or adhesive."
  ],
  "rationale": "According to TMS 402 and BIA Technical Notes, flashing must be continuous, with all joints lapped a minimum of 4 to 6 inches and fully sealed to prevent water infiltration into the building envelope. Unsealed flashing fails to perform its primary function of shedding water to the exterior.",
  "references": [
    "TMS 402",
    "BIA Technical Note 7"
  ]
}
